page.content: The following data table presents a comprehensive overview of asbestos-related materials, specifically focusing on crocidolite, commonly known as blue asbestos. Asbestos has long been recognized for its extensive use in various industrial applications due to its exceptional heat resistance and tensile strength. However, the health risks associated with asbestos exposure, particularly in relation to lung diseases such as asbestosis and mesothelioma, have prompted rigorous scrutiny and regulatory measures. In this context, the table categorizes several characteristics of asbestos, notably detailing metrics such as Average diameter (nm) and Specific surface area (m²/g).

page.content: These metrics are critical for understanding the material's fibrous structure and potential for respiratory toxicity. The Average diameter of fibers can influence their ability to penetrate lung tissue, while the Specific surface area provides insights into the reactivity and potential health hazards posed by the fibers when airborne. The inclusion of this data not only underscores the environmental and health implications of crocidolite but also highlights the necessity for ongoing research and monitoring of asbestos materials to safeguard public health and inform regulatory practices.
